literal_meaning: that taṇhā which leads to renewed becoming
preferred_translation: that ignorant wanting that leads to renewed becoming
alternative_translations: that craving that leads to renewed becoming
definition: Opening segment of the classic four-truth definition of taṇhā.
notes: Use this record when the stock formula is translated as a unit. Keep taṇhā distinct from generic desire-language, preserve the renewed-becoming force of ponobbhavikā, and keep the opening segment coordinated with the following formula members nandirāgasahagatā and tatratatrābhinandinī.

example_phrases.translation: that ignorant wanting that leads to renewed becoming, accompanied by relishing and passion, delighting here and there

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English opening for the classic taṇhā-definition line unless a recorded local reason requires minor grammatical adjustment.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not flatten ponobbhavikā into generic rebirth-language or recast taṇhā as mere desire.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Keeps the opening formula segment aligned with the governed taṇhā family and with renewed-becoming language rather than generic desire or rebirth wording.
